wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the ECS Auto-Stop project overview. Explain the project's purpose as an automated Alibaba Cloud ECS instance management system, its key benefits including cost optimization and resource efficiency, and the overall architecture combining serverless functions with SSH monitoring. Document the technology stack including Alibaba Cloud Function Compute, EventBridge, Table Store, and the agent-based monitoring approach. Include the system's core workflow from SSH connection detection to automatic instance stopping. Provide context for who would benefit from this solution and common use cases in cloud environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: SSH Status Receiver Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the SSH Status Receiver function. Document the HTTP-triggered endpoint (/ssh-status) that accepts POST requests containing SSH connection status data. Explain the authentication mechanism using X-Auth-Token header validation against environment-configured tokens. Detail the input validation process for instance_id, ssh_count, and timestamp fields. Document the Table Store integration for persisting status updates with proper error handling and logging. Include the WSGI handler implementation, request parsing, response formatting, and security validation steps. Provide examples of request/response payloads, error scenarios, and integration patterns with the monitoring agent.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for ECS Auto-Stop. Include prerequisites covering Alibaba Cloud account setup, Terraform installation, and basic serverless concepts. Provide step-by-step installation instructions starting with configuration file setup, infrastructure deployment using deploy.sh, and agent installation on target ECS instances. Document the quick start tutorial with practical examples of initial setup, configuration validation, and first deployment verification. Include common setup issues and their solutions. Make this accessible to beginners while ensuring all necessary technical details are covered.

wiki_catalogs.name: SSH Idle Checker Function
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the SSH Idle Checker function. Document the scheduled trigger processing that periodically checks ECS instance SSH connection status. Explain the threshold-based decision logic for identifying idle instances and determining when to stop them. Detail the ECS API integration for instance management operations including stop/start actions. Document the safety validation mechanisms, rate limiting considerations, and error handling strategies. Include the algorithm for calculating idle time, configuration parameters for thresholds and schedules, and integration with Table Store for status tracking. Provide examples of the decision-making process, logging patterns, and operational procedures for maintenance and troubleshooting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed configuration management documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document the main configuration file structure including Alibaba Cloud region settings, ECS instance identification, Table Store database configuration, Function Compute service settings, and security token management. Explain environment variable configuration for the monitoring agent including connection parameters and reporting intervals. Cover Terraform variable configuration for infrastructure provisioning. Include security best practices for token management, threshold configuration for idle detection and health checks, and notification system setup for DingTalk alerts. Provide configuration examples and validation steps.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive infrastructure architecture documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Describe the Terraform-based infrastructure provisioning including Alibaba Cloud resource creation, IAM role configuration with RAM policies and trust relationships, and network security setup. Document the serverless architecture components including Function Compute services, EventBridge scheduled triggers, and Table Store database provisioning. Explain the resource dependencies, security model with least privilege access, and cost optimization strategies. Include infrastructure topology diagrams showing component relationships and data flow between services. Address scaling considerations and high availability patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed monitoring agent documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document the agent installation process including system requirements, dependency installation, and cron job configuration for periodic SSH status reporting. Explain the agent's core functionality including SSH connection detection, status collection, and secure HTTP reporting to the serverless endpoint. Cover configuration management through environment variables, authentication token setup, and reporting interval customization. Include troubleshooting procedures for common agent issues, log analysis techniques, and manual testing methods. Document the uninstallation process and cleanup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Serverless Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive serverless functions documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document the SSH Status Receiver function including HTTP-triggered endpoint handling, authentication token validation, input sanitization, and Table Store integration for status persistence. Explain the SSH Idle Checker function covering scheduled trigger processing, ECS API integration for instance management, threshold-based decision logic, and safety validation mechanisms. Include function dependencies, error handling strategies, logging patterns, and performance optimization techniques. Document the Python implementation details, AWS Lambda-style runtime behavior, and integration with Alibaba Cloud Function Compute platform.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data management documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document the Table Store schema design including SSH status record structure, timestamp fields, instance identification, and status indicators. Explain data persistence patterns, query operations for status retrieval, and data lifecycle management including retention policies and cleanup procedures. Cover the data flow from agent reporting through function processing to database storage. Include data validation rules, indexing strategies for performance optimization, and backup/recovery considerations. Document data access patterns used by both serverless functions and provide examples of common query operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and access control documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document authentication mechanisms including token-based authentication for agent-server communication, IAM policy configuration with least privilege principles, and trust relationship setup between services. Explain security best practices for token management, credential storage, and secure communication channels. Cover authorization patterns for function access to ECS resources, Table Store data access controls, and network security considerations. Include security audit logging, compliance considerations, and vulnerability assessment guidelines. Document incident response procedures and security monitoring recommendations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment and Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document the deployment process including environment preparation, infrastructure provisioning with Terraform, function package deployment, and post-installation validation. Explain operational procedures including monitoring system health, log analysis techniques, performance tuning, and capacity planning. Cover maintenance operations including function updates, configuration changes, and infrastructure scaling. Include rollback procedures, disaster recovery planning, and backup strategies. Document operational best practices for cost optimization, security hardening, and compliance monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Document common issues including agent installation failures, authentication errors, function execution problems, and infrastructure provisioning issues. Provide systematic debugging procedures for each component including log analysis, status verification, and connectivity testing. Include error message interpretation guides, resolution steps for frequent problems, and diagnostic tools usage. Cover performance troubleshooting including latency issues, timeout problems, and resource utilization optimization. Provide FAQ section addressing frequently asked questions about configuration, deployment, and operational concerns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive advanced topics documentation for ECS Auto-Stop. Cover customization options including threshold parameter adjustment, notification system extensions, and custom authentication methods. Document integration patterns with external monitoring systems, CI/CD pipeline integration, and custom dashboard development. Explain scaling considerations for high-volume deployments, cost optimization strategies, and performance tuning techniques. Include advanced configuration scenarios, multi-region deployment patterns, and disaster recovery implementations. Cover extension points for additional monitoring metrics, custom alerting mechanisms, and integration with third-party services.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# ECS Auto-Stop: Automated Alibaba Cloud ECS Instance Management

## Project Introduction

**ECS Auto-Stop** is a sophisticated automation solution designed to automatically stop idle Alibaba Cloud ECS (Elastic Compute Service) instances to optimize cloud costs. The system leverages a distributed architecture combining cloud-native services to monitor SSH connection activity and intelligently manage instance lifecycle based on usage patterns.

### Purpose Statement
The primary goal is to reduce cloud computing costs by automatically identifying and stopping ECS instances that have been idle for extended periods, while maintaining robust monitoring and alerting capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Cost Optimization**: Automatically stop unused ECS instances to minimize billing
- **Intelligent Monitoring**: Track SSH connection patterns to determine instance activity
- **Reliability**: Implement health checks and fail-safe mechanisms
- **Security**: Enforce authentication and authorization controls
- **Scalability**: Utilize cloud-native services for automatic scaling

### Target Audience
- DevOps engineers managing Alibaba Cloud infrastructure
- Development teams seeking cost optimization solutions
- Organizations with dynamic compute workloads requiring automated instance management

## Technical Architecture

The system follows a microservices architecture leveraging Alibaba Cloud's Function Compute, EventBridge, and Table Store services:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[ECS Instances] --> B[SSH Monitor Agent]
    B --> C[Function Compute HTTP Trigger]
    C --> D[SSH Status Receiver]
    D --> E[Table Store]
    F[EventBridge Scheduler] --> G[Scheduled Trigger]
    G --> H[SSH Idle Checker]
    H --> I[ECS API]
    H --> J[Notification System]
    
    style A fill:#e1f5fe
    style C fill:#f3e5f5
    style D fill:#e8f5e8
    style H fill:#fff3e0
    style I fill:#ffebee
```

### Component Breakdown

**Monitoring Layer**
- SSH Monitor Agent: Lightweight bash script deployed on target ECS instances
- Real-time SSH connection tracking using system utilities
- Periodic reporting mechanism with configurable intervals

**Processing Layer**
- Function Compute Functions: Serverless processing for both monitoring and decision-making
- HTTP-triggered status receiver for real-time updates
- Scheduled-trigger idle checker for periodic monitoring

**Data Layer**
- Table Store: NoSQL database for storing SSH connection status
- Persistent storage of instance activity metrics
- Historical data for trend analysis

**Control Layer**
- EventBridge: Scheduling and orchestration
- RAM policies: Fine-grained access control
- Notification system: Multi-channel alerts

### Design Patterns

**Microservices Pattern**: Each function serves a specific purpose with clear separation of concerns
**Event-Driven Architecture**: Uses scheduled events and HTTP triggers for asynchronous processing
**Serverless Pattern**: Leverages Function Compute for automatic scaling and cost efficiency
**Observability Pattern**: Comprehensive logging and monitoring throughout the system

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Deployment Orchestrator** (`deploy.sh`)
- Terraform-based infrastructure provisioning
- Automated resource creation and configuration
- Environment variable management

**Monitoring Agent** (`ecs-agent/install.sh`, `ecs-agent/report.sh`)
- Bash-based SSH connection monitoring
- Cron-based scheduling for periodic reporting
- Configuration management and validation

### Core Modules

**SSH Status Receiver** (`functions/ssh-status-receiver/index.py`)
- HTTP-triggered function for receiving SSH status reports
- Authentication token validation
- Table Store integration for status persistence
- Input validation and error handling

**SSH Idle Checker** (`functions/ssh-idle-checker/index.py`)
- Scheduled function for idle instance detection
- ECS API integration for instance management
- Threshold-based decision making
- Notification system integration

### Configuration Approach

**Centralized Configuration** (`config/config.yaml.example`)
- Environment-specific settings
- Alibaba Cloud service configurations
- Security and authentication parameters
- Monitoring thresholds and schedules

**Infrastructure as Code** (`infra/main.tf`)
- Terraform module for resource provisioning
- Variable-driven configuration
- Security policy definition
- Output generation for downstream use

### External Dependencies

**Function Dependencies** (`functions/*/requirements.txt`)
- Alibaba Cloud Table Store SDK for Python
- ECS SDK for instance management operations
- OpenAPI SDK for service configuration

**Runtime Dependencies**
- Python 3.9 runtime environment
- Alibaba Cloud Function Compute platform
- EventBridge for scheduling
- Table Store for data persistence

### Integration Points

**Alibaba Cloud Services**
- Function Compute: Serverless execution environment
- EventBridge: Event scheduling and orchestration
- Table Store: NoSQL data storage
- RAM: Identity and access management
- Log Service: Application logging

**External Systems**
- SSH connection monitoring via system utilities
- HTTP endpoints for agent communication
- Notification channels (DingTalk integration)

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Agent as SSH Monitor Agent
    participant HTTP as HTTP Trigger
    participant Receiver as Status Receiver
    participant Store as Table Store
    participant Scheduler as EventBridge
    participant Checker as Idle Checker
    participant ECS as ECS API
    
    Agent->>+HTTP: POST SSH Status
    HTTP->>+Receiver: Process Status
    Receiver->>+Store: Update Status
    Store-->>-Receiver: Acknowledge
    Receiver-->>-HTTP: Success Response
    HTTP-->>-Agent: HTTP 200 OK
    
    Scheduler->>+Checker: Scheduled Trigger
    Checker->>+Store: Read Status
    Store-->>-Checker: Status Data
    Checker->>+ECS: Check Instance Status
    ECS-->>-Checker: Instance Info
    alt Idle Threshold Met
        Checker->>+ECS: Stop Instance
        ECS-->>-Checker: Stop Confirmation
    else Active Instance
        Checker-->>-Scheduler: No Action
    end
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Intelligent Instance Management**
- Automatic detection of idle SSH connections
- Configurable idle thresholds and monitoring intervals
- Real-time status tracking and historical analysis
- Safe operation verification before instance changes

**Robust Monitoring System**
- Multi-layered health checks and validation
- Comprehensive logging and audit trails
- Alerting mechanisms for system issues
- Performance monitoring and metrics collection

**Secure Operations**
- Token-based authentication for agent communication
- Role-based access control with least privilege
- Encrypted credential management
- Audit logging for all operations

### Implementation Highlights

**Agent-Based Monitoring** (`ecs-agent/report.sh`)
- Multi-method SSH connection detection
- Fallback mechanisms for different system configurations
- Configurable reporting intervals
- Error handling and retry logic

**Serverless Processing** (`functions/ssh-idle-checker/index.py`)
- Event-driven architecture for cost efficiency
- Built-in safety checks and validations
- Extensible notification system
- Comprehensive error handling

**Infrastructure Provisioning** (`infra/main.tf`)
- Declarative infrastructure management
- Security-first configuration
- Automated resource cleanup
- Environment-specific customization

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Monitoring
    Monitoring --> Active: SSH Connections Detected
    Monitoring --> Idle: No SSH Connections
    Idle --> Active: SSH Activity Resumes
    Active --> Idle: Extended Inactivity
    Idle --> Stopping: Threshold Reached
    Stopping --> Stopped: Instance Stopped
    Stopped --> Active: Manual Restart
    Stopped --> Monitoring: System Recovery
    
    note right of Monitoring
        Agent reports status
        Check health indicators
    end note
    
    note right of Stopping
        Verify instance status
        Execute stop operation
        Send notifications
    end note
```

**Configuration Management**
- Environment-specific settings
- Security token management
- Threshold customization
- Notification channel setup

**Deployment Automation**
- One-click infrastructure provisioning
- Agent configuration generation
- Resource cleanup and teardown
- Environment validation

**Monitoring and Alerting**
- Real-time status dashboards
- Health check notifications
- Cost optimization insights
- System performance metrics

---

**Sources:**
- [config.yaml.example](config/config.yaml.example)
- [index.py](functions/ssh-idle-checker/index.py)
- [index.py](functions/ssh-status-receiver/index.py)
- [main.tf](infra/main.tf)
- [install.sh](ecs-agent/install.sh)
- [report.sh](ecs-agent/report.sh)
- [deploy.sh](deploy.sh)
- [requirements.txt](functions/ssh-idle-checker/requirements.txt)
- [requirements.txt](functions/ssh-status-receiver/requirements.txt)
- [ram-policy-template.json](infra/ram-policy-template.json)

</blog>
